summary: Restored the decompiled implementation of fighter script handler `ftAction_80071028`, replacing a fully commented-out draft with matching C. The function reads several consecutive command event payloads, updates the command pointer between fixed 4-byte script records, converts packed offsets/ranges by multiplying by `1 / 256.0f`, and spawns fighter GFX through `ftCo_8009F834` unless the fighter is invisible, in which case it skips the GFX spawn via `ftAction_800711DC`. The supporting inline macro `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr` was changed to advance the event pointer by 4 bytes instead of `sizeof(type)`, which appears central to matching this command-stream layout.
what_changed: Uncommented and restored `ftAction_80071028` in `src/melee/ft/ftaction.c` as active C code.
Changed fighter access from the commented draft's direct `gobj->user_data` idea to `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`.
Implemented invisible-fighter handling: if `fp->invisible` is true, the function calls `ftAction_800711DC(gobj, cmd)` to skip the GFX spawn event.
Implemented bone selection from either `fp->ft_data->x8->x12` when `cmd->u->test1.useUnkBone` is set, or `cmd->u->test1.boneId` otherwise.
Read `useCommonBoneIDs`, `destroyOnStateChange`, `gfxID`, `unkFloat`, offset components, and range components from consecutive `CommandInfo` union payload views named `test1` through `test5`.
Inserted repeated `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr(new_var->u, struct testN)` calls between reads to advance through the packed event stream.
Changed `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr(event, type)` in `src/melee/ft/inlines.h` from advancing by `sizeof(type)` to advancing by a fixed 4 bytes.
Kept nested block structure and a `CommandInfo* new_var` temporary, apparently to satisfy matching/permuter constraints noted by the inline comment `// permuter jank?`.
smart_moves: Used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` rather than open-coding `gobj->user_data`, aligning with existing fighter access conventions.
Preserved a dedicated `new_var` temporary and nested scopes so local variable lifetimes/order could be shaped for matching without exposing unnecessary state across the whole function.
Recognized that the script event pointer advances in 4-byte command units, not by the apparent C struct size, and fixed the shared macro accordingly.
Kept the invisible case as an explicit skip path to `ftAction_800711DC`, matching the intended behavior documented in the old commented draft.
Grouped the final call to `ftCo_8009F834` only after all packed command fields are read and scaled, preserving the event parsing order.
decomp_lessons: For Melee command/script streams, C struct names used to interpret command payloads may not represent the actual pointer stride; verify whether the engine advances by fixed command words such as 4 bytes rather than `sizeof(struct)`.
When restoring commented decomp drafts, small header macro semantics can be the key blocker for making the function match.
Nested scopes can be a practical matching tactic to constrain variable lifetimes and stack/register allocation in old CodeWarrior output.
A temporary pointer assignment inside a macro call, such as `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr((new_var = cmd)->u, struct test1)`, can help reproduce original evaluation/order behavior.
Fixed-point script values may need explicit scaling by `(1 / 256.0f)` when producing runtime `Vec3` offsets and ranges.
Preserve behavior for skipped command effects: this function does not merely return for invisible fighters, it delegates to the next action handler to skip the GFX spawn event.
naming_conventions: `ftAction_80071028` and `ftAction_800711DC` retain address-based names, indicating unidentified or partially named fighter action/script handlers.
`ftCo_8009F834` follows the fighter common prefix convention `ftCo_` plus address.
`GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the preferred access macro for retrieving `Fighter*` from a fighter GObj.
Command payload union members in this area are still provisional names such as `test1`, `test2`, `test3`, `test4`, and `test5`.

Field names such as `useUnkBone`, `useCommonBoneIDs`, `destroyOnStateChange`, `gfxID`, `offsetX/Y/Z`, and `rangeX/Y/Z` describe interpreted command bits/fields but still sit inside provisional command structs.
The header itself retains a `@todo Fix naming` comment near `gmScriptEventCast` and `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr`, so naming around script event helpers is explicitly not final.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed pointer advancement from `sizeof(type)` to a literal `+ 4`, likely matching the actual script command stream stride and generated arithmetic.
Used nested blocks around local declarations (`bone`, `use_common_bone_id`, `destroy_on_state_change`, `gfx_id`) to influence local lifetime and codegen.
Declared `float unk`, `Vec3 offset`, and `Vec3 range` near the top while keeping integer locals in inner scopes, a common decomp tactic for stack/register matching.
Kept the suspicious `new_var` temporary noted by `// permuter jank?`, suggesting the exact temporary is needed for instruction scheduling or register allocation.
Used explicit `(1 / 256.0f) * value` expressions for fixed-point conversion rather than alternative equivalent constants or division forms, likely to preserve float codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human text excerpt is empty, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or replace provisional command structs `test1` through `test5`.
Audit other uses of `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr` to confirm the fixed 4-byte stride is valid for all script event parsing sites.
Search for additional restored `ftAction_*` handlers that parse `CommandInfo` streams and reuse the same nested-scope matching tactic.
Check whether `fp->ft_data->x8->x12` later receives a semantic field name related to unknown/default bone IDs.
Look for review or issue discussion outside the provided excerpt explaining why `sizeof(type)` was incorrect for `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr`.
